path-of-the-beast.name: Path of the Beast
desc: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Beast draw their rage from a bestial spark burning within their souls. Such a barbarian might be inhabited by a primal spirit or be descended from shape-shifters. That beast bursts forth in the throes of rage, physically transforming the barbarian.

3.name: Form of the Beast
3.desc: Whenever you enter your rage, you can transform, revealing the bestial power within you. Until the rage ends, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you may add your STR modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it, as normal. You choose the weapon's form each time you rage:

content.name: Bite
content.desc: Your mouth transforms into a bestial muzzle or great mandibles. It deals 1d8 piercing damage on a hit. Once per turn, when you damage a creature with this bite, you regain a number of hitpoints equal to your proficiency bonus, provided you have less than half your hitpoints when you hit.

content.name: Claws
content.desc: Each of your hands transforms into a claw, which you may use as a weapon if it's empty. It deals 1d6 slashing damage on a hit. Once per turn, when you attack with a claw using the attack action, you may make one additional claw attack as part of the same action.

content.name: Tail
content.desc: You grow a lashing, spiny tail, which deals 1d8 piercing damage on a hit, and has the reach property. If a creature you can see within 10ft. of you hits you with an attack, you may use your reaction to swipe your tail and roll 1d8, adding the number rolled to your AC, potentially causing the attack to miss you.

6.name: Bestial Soul
6.desc: The feral power within you increases, causing the natural weapons of your Form of the Beast feature to count as magical. You may also alter your form to help you adapt to your surroundings. Whenever you finish a short rest, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until the next short rest.

content.desc: You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed, and you can breathe underwater.
You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Whenever you jump, you may make an Athletics (STR) check and extend your jump by a number of ft. equal to the check's total. You may make this check only once per turn.

10.name: Infectious Fury
10.desc: Whenever you hit a creature with your natural weapons while you are raging, the beast within you can curse your target with rabid fury. The target must succeed on a WIS saving throw (DC equal to 8 + CON + proficiency). On a failure, you may choose to force the target to use its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature of your choice that you can see, or to have the target take 2d12 @psychic damage. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus every long rest.

14.name: Call the Hunt
14.desc: Whenever you enter you rage, you may choose a number of willing creatures you can see within 30ft. of you equal to your CON (minimum of 1). You gain 5 temporary hitpoints for each creature that accepts this feature. Until the rage ends, the chosen creatures gain 1d6 bonus damage on attack rolls once per turn.

college-of-glamour.name: College of Glamour
desc: The College of Glamour is the home of bards who mastered their craft in the vibrant realm of the Feywild or under the tutelage of someone who dwelled there. Tutored by satyrs, eladrin, and other fey, these bards learn to use their magic to delight and captivate others.

The bards of this college are regarded with a mixture of awe and fear. Their performances are the stuff of legend. These bards are so eloquent that a speech or song that one of them performs can cause captors to release the bard unharmed and can lull a furious dragon into complacency. The same magic that allows them to quell beasts can also bend minds. Villainous bards of this college can leech off a community for weeks, abusing their magic to turn their hosts into thralls. Heroic bards of this college instead use this power to gladden the downtrodden and undermine oppressors.

3.name: Mantle of Inspiration
3.desc: As a bonus action, you may expend one use of your bardic inspiration to grant yourself a wondrous appearance. When you do so, choose a number of creatures you can see and who can see you within 60ft. of you, up to a number equal to your CHA modifier, minimum of 1. Each of them gains 5 temporary hitpoints. When a creature gains these temporary hitpoints, it can immediately use its reaction to move up to its movement speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. The amount of temporary hitpoints gained from this feature increases to 8 at 5th level, 11 at 10th level and 14 at 15th level.

6.name: Mantle of Majesty
6.desc: You gain the ability to cloak yourself in a fey magic that makes others want to serve you. As a bonus action, you may cast the @command spell, without expending a spell slot. When you do, you take on an appearance of unearthly beauty for 1 minute, or until your concentration ends, as if you were concentrating on a spell. During this time, you may cast @command as a bonus action on each of your turns, without expending a spell slot. Any creature charmed by you automatically fails its saving throw against the @command spell you cast with this feature. You may use this feature once per long rest.

14.name: Unbreakable Majesty
14.desc: Your appearance permanently gains an otherworldy aspect that makes you look more lovely and fierce. As a bonus action, you may assume a magically majestic prescence for 1 minute, or until you are incapacitated. For the duration, whenever any creature tries to attack you for the first time on a turn, the attacker must make a CHA saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it cannot attack you on this turn, and it must choose a new target for its attack, or the attack is wasted. On a successful save, it may attack you on this turn, but it has disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against your spells on your next turn. You may use this feature once per short rest.

college-of-valor.name: College of Valor
desc: Bards of the College of Valor are daring skalds whose tales keep alive the memory of the great heroes of the past, and thereby inspire a new generation of heroes. These bards gather in mead halls or around great bonfires to sing the deeds of the mighty, both past and present. They travel the land to witness great events firsthand and to ensure that the memory of those events doesn't pass from the world. With their songs, they inspire others to reach the same heights of accomplishment as the heroes of old.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.desc: You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

3.name: Combat Inspiration
3.desc: You've learned to inspire others in battle. A creature that has a bardic inspiration die from you may roll that die and add the number rolled to a weapon damage roll it just made. Alternatively, when an attack roll is made against the creature, it may use its reaction to roll the die and add the number to its AC against that attack. It may do this after seeing the roll, but before knowing whether it hits or misses.

6.name: Extra Attack
6.desc: You may attack twice instead of once whenever you take the @attack action on your turn.

14.name: Battle Magic
14.desc: You have mastered the art of weaving spellcasting and weapon use into a single, harmonious act. When you use your action to cast a bard spell, as a bonus action, you may make a weapon attack.

circle-of-the-moon.name: Circle of the Moon
desc: Druids of the Circle of the Moon are fierce guardians of the wilds. Their order gathers under the full moon to share news and trade warnings. They haunt the deepest parts of the wilderness, where they might go for weeks on end before crossing paths with another humanoid creature, let alone another druid. Changeable as the moon, a druid of this circle might prowl as a great cat one night, soar over the treetops as an eagle the next day, and crash through the undergrowth in bear form to drive off a trespassing monster. The wild is in the druid's blood.

2.name: Combat Wild Shape
2.desc: You gain the ability to use your Wild Shape feature as a bonus action, rather than as an action. While in beast form, you may use a bonus action to expend 1 spell slot to regain 1d8 hitpoints per level of the spell slot consumed.

2.name: Circle Forms
2.desc: The rites of your circle grant you the ability to transform into more dangerous animal forms. You may use your Wild Shape feature to transform into a beast with a CR as high as 1. You ignore the max CR your druid class would normally allow, but must abide by the other limitations there.

6.name: Primal Strike
6.desc: Your attacks in beast form count as magical.

6.name: Circle Forms
6.desc: The max CR of beast you are allowed to transform into with your Wild Shape feature is equal to your druid level divided by 3 (rounded down).

10.name: Elemental Wild Shape
10.desc: You may expend two uses of your Wild Shape feature at the same time to transform into an air, earth, fire or water elemental.

14.name: Thousand Forms
14.desc: You have learned to use magic to alter your physical form in more subtle ways. You may cast the @alter self spell at will.

oath-of-devotion.name: Oath of Devotion
desc: The Oath of Devotion binds a paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue and order. Sometimes called cavaliers, white knights or holy warriors, these paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor, acting with honor in pursuit of justice and the greater good. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to the same standards. Many who swear this oath are devoted to gods of law and good, and use their gods' tenents as the measure of their devotion. They hold angels - the perfect servants of good - as their ideals, and incorporate images of angelic wings into their helmets or coats of arms.

3.name: Oath Spells
3.desc: You gain access to the following spells at the specified levels. You always have them prepared, and they do not count against the number of spells you can prepare:

3.name: Tenets of Devotion
3.desc: Though the exact words and strictures of the Oath of Devotion vary, paladins of this oath share these tenets:

content.name: Honesty
content.desc: Don't lie or cheat. Let your word be your promise.

content.name: Courage
content.desc: Never fear to act, though caution is wise.

content.name: Compassion
content.desc: Aid others, protect the weak, and punish those who threaten them. Show mercy to your foes, but temper it with wisdom.

content.name: Honor
content.desc: Treat others with fairness, and let your honorable deeds be an example to them. Do as much good as possible while causing the least amount of harm.

content.name: Duty
content.desc: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you.

3.name: Channel Divinity
3.desc: You gain the following 2 Channel Divinity options:

content.name: Sacred Weapon
content.desc: As an action, you may imbue one weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 1 minute, you may add your CHA modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum of +1). The weapon also emits bright light in a 20ft. radius, and dim light in another 20ft. If the weapon is not already magical, it becomes magical for the duration. The effect ends if you dismiss it as a free action, you stop holding the weapon or are knocked unconscious.

content.name: Turn the Unholy
content.desc: As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer, censuring fiends and undead. Each fiend or undead that can see or hear you within 30ft. of you must make a WIS saving throw. On a failure, the creature is turned for 1 minute, or until it takes damage. A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it cannot willingly move to a space within 30ft. of you. It also cannot take reactions. For its action, it can use only the @dash action, or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, the creature may instead use the @dodge action.

7.name: Aura of Devotion
7.desc: You and friendly creatures within 10ft. of you cannot be charmed while you are conscious.

15.name: Purity of Spirit
15.desc: You are permanently under the effects of a protection from evil and good spell.

20.name: Holy Nimbus
20.desc: As an action, you may emenate an aura of sunlight. For 1 minute, bright light shines from you in a 30ft. radius, and dim light in another 30ft. Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn in the bright light, the creature takes 10 @radiant damage. Additionally, for the duration, you gain advantage on saving throws against spells cast by fiends or undead. You may use this feature once per long rest.

way-of-the-astral-self.name: Way of the Astral Self
desc: A monk who follows the Way of the Astral Self believes their body is an illusion. They see their ki as a representation of their true form, an astral self. This astral self has the capacity to be a force of order or disorder, with some monasteries training students to use their power to protect the weapon, and others instructing aspirants in how to manifest their true selves in service of the mighty.

3.name: Arms of the Astral Self
3.desc: As a bonus action, you may spend 1 ki point to summon the arms of your astral self. When you do so, each creature of your choice that you can see within 10ft. of you must succeed on a DEX saving throw or take force damage equal to two rolls of your martial arts die. For the next 10 minutes, these spectral arms hover near either your shoulders or surrounding your arms. You may determine the appearance of these arms, and they will vanish early if you are incapacitated or if you die. The arms grant you the following traits:

content.desc: You may use your WIS modifier instead of your STR modifier when making STR checks and saving throws.

You may use the spectral arms to make unarmed strikes. You may use your WIS modifier instead of your STR modifier for the attack and damage rolls of these strikes, and their damage type is force damage. Additionally, your reach for these unarmed strikes increases by 5ft.

6.name: Visage of the Astral Self
6.desc: You gain the ability to summon the visage of your astral self. As a bonus action or as part of the bonus action you take to activate the arms of the astral self trait, you may spend 1 ki point to summon this visage for 10 minutes. It will vanish early if you are incapacitated or if you die. The visage covers your face like a helmet or mask. You may determine its appearance. The visage grants you the following traits:

content.name: Astral Sight
content.desc: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120ft.

content.name: Wisdom of the Spirit
content.desc: You gain advantage on Insight (WIS) and Intimidation (CHA) checks.

content.name: Astral Sight
content.desc: When you speak, you may direct your words to a creature of your choice within 60ft. of you, so that only they may hear you. Alternatively, you may amplify your voice so that all creatures within up to 600ft. can hear you.

11.name: Body of the Astral Self
11.desc: When you have both your astral arms and visage summoned, you may cause the body of your astral self to appear. This requires no action. This spectral body covers your physical form like a suit of armor, connecting to the arms and visage. You may determine its appearance. The body grants you the following traits:

content.name: Deflect Energy
content.desc: Whenever you take @acid, @cold, @fire, @force, @lightning or @thunder damage, you may use your reaction to deflect it. When you do so, the damage you take is reduced by 1d10 + WIS. The damage is reduced by a minimum of 1.

content.name: Empowered Arms
content.desc: Once on each of your turns, whenever you hit you a target with an unarmed strike from the astral arms, you may deal extra damage equal to your martial arts die.

17.name: Awakened Astral Self
17.desc: Your connection to your astral self is complete. As a bonus action, you may spend 5 ki points to summon your full astral body (arms, visage and body), and awaken it for 10 minutes. The awakening ends if you are incapacitated or if you die. The awakened astral self gives you the following traits:

content.name: Armor of the Spirit
content.desc: You gain +2 AC.

content.name: Astral Barrage
content.desc: Whenever you use the monk's extra attack feature to attack twice, you may instead attack three times if all the attacks are unarmed strikes with your astral arms.

way-of-the-drunken-master.name: Way of the Drunken Master
desc: The Way of the Drunken Master teaches its students to move with the jerky, unpredictable movements of a drunkard. A drunken master sways, tottering on unsteady feet, to present what seems like an incompetent combatent who proves frustrating to engage. The drunken master's erratic stumbles conceal a carefully executed dance of blocks, parries, advances, attacks and retreats.

A drunken master often enjoys playing the fool to bring gladness to the despondent or to demonstrate humility to the arrogant, but when battle is joined, the drunken master can be a maddening, masterful foe.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.desc: You gain proficiency in the Performance (CHA) skill if you don't already have it. You also gain proficiency with brewer's supplies if you don't already have it.

3.name: Drunken Technique
3.desc: Whenever you use your flurry of blows trait, you gain the benefit of the @disengage action, and your walking speed increases by 10ft. until the end of that turn.

content.name: Leap to Your Feet
content.desc: When you're prone, you may stand up by spending 5ft. of movement, rather than the usual amount.

content.name: Redirect Attack
content.desc: Whenever a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you may spend 1 ki point as a reaction to cause that attack to hit one creature of your choice, other than the attacker, that you can see within 5ft. of you.

11.name: Drunkard's Luck
11.desc: Whenever you make an ability check, an attack roll, or a saving throw and have disadvantage, you may spend 2 ki points to remove the disadvantage for that roll.

17.name: Intoxicated Frenzy
17.desc: When you use your flurry of blows, you may make up to 3 additional attacks with it, for a total of 5, but only if each attack targets different creatures this turn.

arcane-trickster.name: Arcane Trickster
desc: Some rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with magic, learning tricks of enchantment and illusion. These rogues include pickpockets and burglars, but also pranksters, mischief-makers, and a significant number of adventurers.

3.name: Spellcasting
3.desc: You augment your martial prowess with the ability to cast spells.

content.name: Cantrips
content.desc: You learn 3 cantrips: @mage hand, and 2 other cantrips of your choice from the wizard spell list. You learn 1 additional cantrip at 10th level.

content.name: Known Spells
content.desc: You know 3 1st-level spells of your choice, 2 of which you must choose from the enchantment and illusion school on the wizard spell list. Any new spells you learn must also come from these 2 schools, unless otherwise specified. The Spells Known column of the arcane trickster spell slot table shows how many spells you know at any given level. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you may choose one of the spells you know, and replace it with any other spell. Each spell you learn can only be of a spell slot level you have.

content.name: Spellcasting Ability
content.desc: Your spellcasting ability is INT.

content.name: Spell Save DC
content.desc: 8 + proficiency + INT

content.name: Spell attack modifier
content.desc: proficiency + INT

3.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
3.desc: Whenever you cast the mage hand cantrip, you may make the spectral hand invisible, and you may perform additional tasks with it, such as stowing or retrieving an object in a container worn or carried by another creature, or pick locks or disarm traps at range. You may perform one of these tasks without being noticed by a creature if you succeed on a Sleight of Hand (DEX) check, contested by the creature's Perception (WIS) check. You may use a bonus action to control the hand.

9.name: Magical Ambush
9.desc: If you are hidden from a creature when you cast a spell on it, the creature has disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against the spell this turn.

13.name: Versatile Trickster
13.desc: You gain the ability to distract targets with your @mage hand. As a bonus action, you may designate a creature within 5ft. of the spectral hand created by the spell. Doing so gives you advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your turn.

17.name: Spell Thief
17.desc: You gain the ability to magically steal the knowledge of how to cast a spell from another spellcaster. Immediately after a creature casts a spell that targets you, or includes you in its area of effect, you may use your reaction to force the creature to make a saving throw with its spellcasting ability modifier. The DC equals your spell save DC. On a failure, you negate the spell's effect against you, and you steal the knowledge of the spell if it is at least 1st level, and of a level you can cast. It does not have to be a wizard spell. For the next 8 hours, you know this spell, and may cast it using your spell slots. The creature you stole the spell from cannot cast this spell until these 8 hours have passed. You may use this feature once per long rest.

assassin.name: Assassin
desc: You focus your training on the grim art of death. Those who adhere to this archetype are diverse: hired killers, spies, bounty hunters, and even specially anointed priests trained to exterminate the enemies of their deity. Stealth, poison, and disguise help you eliminate your foes with deadly efficiency.

3.name: Bonus Proficiencies
3.desc: You gain proficiency with the disguise kit and the poisoner's kit.

3.name: Assassinate
3.desc: You are at your deadliest when you get the drop on your enemies. You gain advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in this combat yet. Additionally, any hit you score against a creature that is surprised is guaranteed a critical hit.

9.name: Infiltration Expertise
9.desc: You may unfailingly create false identities for yourself. You must spend 7 days and 25gp to establish the history, profession, and affiliations for an identity. You cannot establish an identity that belongs to someone else. For example, you might acquire appropriate clothing, letters of introduction, and official-looking certification to establish yourself as a member of a trading house from a remote city so you can insinuate yourself into the company of other wealthy merchants. Thereafter, if you adopt the new identity as a disguise, other creatures believe you to be that person until given an obvious reason not to.

13.name: Impostor
13.desc: You gain the ability to unerringly mimic another person's speech, writing and behavior. You must spend at least 3 hours studying these three components of the person's behavior, listening to speech, examining handwriting and observing mannerisms. Your ruse is indiscernable to the casual observer. If a wary creature suspects something is amiss, you gain advantage on any Deception (CHA) check you make to avoid detection.

17.name: Death Strike
17.desc: You become a master of instant death. When you attack and hit a creature that is surprised, it must succeed on a CON saving throw (DC 8 + your DEX + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, the damage your attack deals against the creature is doubled.

thief.name: Thief
desc: You hone your skills in the larcenous arts. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype, but so do rogues who prefer to think of themselves as professional treasure seekers, explorers, delvers, and investigators. In addition to improving your agility and stealth, you learn skills useful for delving into ancient ruins, reading unfamiliar languages, and using magic items you normally couldn't employ.

3.name: Fast Hands
3.desc: You may use the bonus action granted by your cunning action trait for the following actions. You may:

content.desc: Make a Sleight of Hand (DEX) check.
Use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock.
Take the @use an object action.

3.name: Second-Story Work
3.desc: Climbing no longer costs you extra movement. Additionally, when you make a running jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of ft. equal to your DEX.

9.name: Supreme Sneak
9.desc: You have advantage on Stealth (DEX) checks if you move no more than half your speed that turn.

13.name: Use Magic Device
13.desc: You may ignore all class, race and level requirements on the use of magic items.

17.name: Thief's Reflexes
17.desc: On the first round of any combat, you may make two turns. You take your first turn at your normal initiative, and your second turn at your initiative - 10. You cannot use this feature if you are surprised.

shadow-magic.name: Shadow Magic
desc: You are a creature of shadow, for your innate magic comes from the Shadowfell itself. You might trace your lineage to an entity from that place, or perhaps you were exposed to its fell energy and transformed by it. The power of shadow magic casts a strange pall over your physical presence. The spark of life that sustains you is muffled, as if it struggles to remain viable against the dark energy that imbues your soul.

1.name: Quirk (Optional)
1.desc: You may pick from - or roll on - the following table to create a quirk for your character:

content: You are always icy cold to the touch.
When you are asleep, you don't appear to breathe (though you mst still breathe to survive).
You barely bleed, even when badly injured.
Your heart beats once per minute. This event sometimes surprises you.
You have trouble remembering that living creatures and corpses should be treated differently.

1.name: Eyes of the Dark
1.desc: You gain 120ft. darkvision. At 3rd level, you learn the @darkness spell, which doesn't count against your number of sorcerer spells known. You may cast this spell by spending 2 sorcery points, or by casting a spell slot as normal. If you cast it with sorcery points, you are able to see through the darkness created by the spell.

1.name: Strength of the Grave
1.desc: When damage reduces you to 0 hitpoints, you may make a CHA saving throw (DC 5 + the damage taken). On a success, you instead drop to 1 hitpoint. You cannot use this feature if you are reduced to 0 hitpoints by either @radiant damage or a critical hit. After this saving throw succeeds, you cannot use this feature again until after a long rest.

6.name: Hound of Ill Omen
6.desc: You gain the ability to call forth a howling creature of darkness to harass your foes. As a bonus action, you may spend 3 sorcery points to summon a hound of ill omen to target one creature you can see within 120ft. of you. The hound uses the #dire wolf's stat block, with the following properties:

content.desc: The hound is size Medium instead of Large, and counts as a Monstrosity instead of a Beast.
It appears with a number of temporary hitpoints equal to half your sorcerer level.
It may move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. The hound takes 5 @force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.
At the start of its turn, the hound automatically knows its target's location. If the target was hidden, it is no longer hidden from the hound.
The hound appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30ft. of the target.

The hound has its own initiative. On its turn, it can move only toward its target by the most direct route, and it can use its action only to attack the target. The hound is able to make opportunity attacks, but only against its target.
While the hound is within 5ft. of the target, the target has disadvantage on saving throws against any spell you cast.
The hound disappears if it is reduced to 0 hitpoints, if its target is reduced to 0 hitpoints, or after 5 minutes.

10.name: Shadow Walk
10.desc: When you are in dim light or darkness, as a bonus action, you are able to teleport up to 120ft. to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness.

14.name: Umbral Form
14.desc: As a bonus action, you may spend 6 sorcery points to transform yourself into a shadowy form. In this form, you gain resistance to all damage except @force and @radiant damage, and you may move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. You take 5 @force damage if you end your turn inside an object. This form lasts for 1 minute, until you are incapacitated or die, or if you dismiss it as a bonus action.

the-catalyst.name: The Catalyst
desc: Stumbling upon ancient ruins, or research of ancient texts and scripts, you stumbled upon a seemingly well-maintained crypt, housing an uncannily unblemished statue of gold grandeur. Equally uncanny, it granted you an enticing offer: an eternal life of power. Either by accident, or by choice, you granted access to your body and soul to C'ta-rizh the Accumulator, a devilish entity older than script and man, more knowledgeable than many a deity. It bides its time - waiting, collecting, preserving - to achieve its goals, now with you in its ranks, whether you want to or not.

1.name: Expanded Spell List
1.desc: The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you:

1.name: Mechanized Infantry
1.desc: Through this pact with the Preserver, you have become partially mechanical. Your body gains the following properties:

content.desc: Your soul is not fully present, and your body has been put in a form of industrial stasis. You cannot age – naturally, nor magically – and cannot die of old age.

Your heart has been replaced by a magical energy core. This core may act as the arcane focus for your Warlock spells. Losing access to magic – be it through an antimagic field, the wish spell, divine intervention, or other means – will dispel your heart for the duration, and cause you to fall unconscious. Instantaneous spells and effects will stop your heart momentarily, causing you to fall prone. A spell needs to be of a level equal to half your Warlock level, rounded up, to successfully dispel your heart. If you are unconscious in this way for more than 1d4 + 1 days in succession, you will die.
You gain resistance against @psychic damage.
Additionally, choose one of the following enhancements:

content.name: Chest
content.desc: You gain +1 to CON saving throws, and +1 AC.

content.name: Legs
content.desc: You gain +1 to DEX saving throws, and +10ft. movement speed.

content.name: Eyes
content.desc: You gain +1 to WIS saving throws, and 30ft. of darkvision. If you already have darkvision, the range of your darkvision increases by 30ft. instead.

1.name: Enlister, Constructor
1.desc: When you kill a creature, you may use your reaction to constrict their soul into steel, enlisting them into your master's legions as your servant. The creature is resurrected into an automaton. Automatons have their own initiative. An automaton takes 10gp per week to maintain, or it'll break down. A destroyed automaton will turn to dust. You may have one automaton at a time. If you attempt to make more automatons than you are able to, the oldest automaton will turn to dust. Statblock will be added later.

6.name: Rite of Preservation
6.desc: As an action, you may allow your patron to partially possess your neural networks. You transform: your nerves branch into steel, your hair (if you have it) drapes into fine copper wires, your bones become adamantine rods, and your skin turns into a bronzed alloy sheet. Your body hardens, and you become an immovable statue. You may use this trait once every long rest. For 1 minute, you gain the following effects:

content.desc: Your speed becomes 0ft., you cannot benefit from any bonus to your speed, and you cannot make weapon attacks.

Your form becomes absolute, and you cannot be affected by spells that would change your form or forcibly move/tilt your body (such as the @polymorph, @enlarge/reduce or @gust spells), unless of 5th level or higher. A creature attempting to move your body must succeed on a STR check with a DC equal to 2 times your AC.
Weapon and spell attacks that hit but are within 5 of your AC deal damage as if you have resistance to the damage. You gain advantage to CON saving throws, but disadvantage to DEX saving throws.

You lose all your regular senses, including sight, hearing and smell, instead gaining blindsight with a range equal to the range of your darkvision, or 60ft. (whichever is larger), as well as 20ft. truesight. For the duration, you don't need to breathe, but cannot speak.
You may ignore somatic and verbal components for your Warlock spells.

Once on your turn, you may use your bonus action to empower a creature within 30ft. of you. The next time that creature casts a spell in the next round, the spell's effects increase as if it was cast 1 level higher.
You may cast the @magic missile spell once per turn, at 1st level, without expending a spell slot.

10.name: Uniformity
10.desc: You may communicate telepathically with any construct within 30ft. of you. Constructs are generally biased to view you as friendly. You gain advantage on CHA skill checks made against constructs.

10.name: Rite of Preservation
10.desc: You gain the following additional effects while transformed:

content.desc: You may use this trait 2 times every long rest. Every usage past the first results in a weakened rite, preventing the use of bonus actions and reactions.
You gain the ability to telepathically communicate with a number of non-construct creatures up to your CHA modifier. This has a range of 120ft.
You may call upon your patron to further solidify your outer shell. You may expend 1 spell slot and gain temporary hitpoints equal to 5 times the level of the spell slot.
You cannot be affected by spells that would change your form or forcibly move/tilt your body, unless of 7th level or higher.

10.name: Enlister, Constructor
10.desc: You may maintain 2 automatons at a time, instead of 1.

14.name: Rite of Preservation
14.desc: You gain the following additional effects:

content.desc: You may use this trait 3 times every long rest. Every usage past the first results in a weakened rite, preventing the use of bonus actions and reactions.

At the start of the rite, choose up to 3 spells of 5th level or lower. While the rite lasts, you and the automatons from your enlister, constructor feature gain immunity to the damage and effects of these spells.
You cannot be affected by spells that would change your form or forcibly move/tilt your body, unless of 9th level.

the-hexblade.name: The Hexblade
desc: You have made your pact with a mysterious entity from the Shadowfell - a force that manifests in sentient magic weapons carved from the stuff of shadow. The mighty sword Blackrazor is the most notable of these weapons, which have been spread across the multiverse over the ages. The shadowy force behind these weapons can offer power to warlocks who form pacts with it. Many hexblade warlocks create weapons that emulate those formed in the Shadowfell. Others forgo such arms, content to weave the dark magic of that plane into their spellcasting.

Because the Raven Queen is known to have forged the first of these weapons, many sages speculate that she and the force are one and that the weapons, along with hexblade warlocks, are tools she uses to manipulate events on the Material Plane to her inscrutable ends.

1.name: Expanded Spell List
1.desc: The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you:

1.name: Hex Warrior
1.desc: You acquire the training necessary to effectively arm yourself in battle, gaining proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons. The influence of your patron also allows you to mystically channel your will through a particular weapon. Whenever you finish a long rest, you may touch one weapon that you are proficient with and that lacks the two-handed property. When you attack with that weapon, you may use your CHA instead of STR or DEX for the attack and damage rolls. This benefit lasts until you finish a long rest. If you get the pact of the blade, this feature works on every weapon you conjure with that feature, regardless of weapon type.

1.name: Hexblade's Curse
1.desc: As a bonus action, you may place a baleful curse on someone. Choose one creature you can see within 30ft. of you. The target is cursed for 1 minute, but ends early if the target dies, or you are incapacitated. You may use this feature once per short rest. For the duration, you gain the following benefits:

content.desc: You gain a bonus to damage rolls equal to your proficiency bonus against the cursed target.
Any attack roll of 19 or 20 on the d20 against the cursed target is a critical hit.
When the cursed target dies, you regain hitpoints equal to your warlock level + CHA (minimum of 1).

6.name: Accursed Specter
6.desc: When you kill a humanoid, you may curse its soul and cause its spirit to rise from its corpse as a specter, the statistics of which are in the Monster Manual. When the specter appears, it gains temporary hitpoints equal to half your warlock level. Roll initiative for the specter, which gains its own turns. It obeys your verbal commands, and gains a bonus to its attack rolls equal to your CHA modifier. The specter remains in your service until the end of your next long rest, at which point it vanishes to the afterlife. You may use this feature once per long rest.

10.name: Armor of Hexes
10.desc: Your hex grows more powerful. If the target cursed by your hexblade's curse feature hits you with an attack roll, you may use your reaction to roll 1d2. On a 2, the attack instead misses you, regardless of its roll.

14.name: Master of Hexes
14.desc: You may spread your hexblade's curse from creature to creature. Whenever the creature cursed by your hexblade's curse feature dies, you may apply the curse to a different creature you can see within 30ft. of you, provided you aren't incapacitated. When you apply the curse in this way, you do not regain hitpoints from the death of the previously cursed creature.

the-fiend.name: The Fiend
desc: You have made a pact with a fiend from the lower plains of existence, a being whose aims are evil, even if you strive against those aims. Such beings desire the corruption of all things, ultimately including you. Fiends powerful enough to form a pact include demon lords such as Demogorgon, Oruc, Fraz'Urb-luu and Baphomet; archdevils such as Asmodeus, Dispater, Mephistopheles and Belial; pit fiends and balors that are especially mighty and ultroloths and other lords of the yugoloths.

1.name: Expanded Spell List
1.desc: The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you:

1.name: Dark One's Blessing
1.desc: When you reduce any creature of your choice to 0 hitpoints, you may gain temporary hitpoints equal to your CHA + your warlock level (minimum of 1)

6.name: Dark One's Own Luck
6.desc: Whenever you make an ability check or a saving throw, you may use this trait to add 1d10 to your roll. You may do so after seeing the initial roll, but before any of the roll's effects occur. You may use this trait once every short rest.

10.name: Fiendish Resilience
10.desc: After finishing a short rest, you may choose one damage type. You gain resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this trait. Magical weapons and silver weapons ignore this resistance.

14.name: Hurl Through Hell
14.desc: Whenever you hit a creature with an attack, you may use this feature to instantly transport the target through the 9 hells. The creature disappears and hurtles through a nightmarish landscape. At the end of your next turn, the target returns to the nearest unoccupied space to where it previously was. If the target is not a fiend, the target takes 10d10 @psychic damage. You may use this trait once every long rest.

bladesinging.name: Bladesinging
desc: Bladesingers master a tradition of wizardry that incorporates swordplay and dance. Originally created by elves, this tradition has been adopted by non-elf practicioners, who honor and expand on the elven ways. In combat, a bladesinger uses a series of intricate, elegant maneuvers that fend off harm and allow the bladesinger to channel magic into devastating attacks and a cunning defense. Many who have observed a bladesinger at work remember the display as one of the more beautiful experiences in their life, a glorious dance accompanied by a single blade.

2.name: Training in War and Song
2.desc: You gain proficiency with light armor, and one type of melee weapon that does not have the two-handed property.

2.name: Bladesong
2.desc: You may invoke an elven magic called the Bladesong, provided you aren't wearing medium or heavy armor, or using a shield. It graces you with supernatural speed, agility and focus. You may use a bonus action to start the song, which lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if you are incapacitated, if you don medium or heavy armor or a shield, or if you use two hands to make an attack with a weapon. You may also dismiss the song at any time as a free action. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus every long rest. While the song is active, you gain the following benefits.

content.desc: You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your INT (minimum of 1).
Your walking speed increases by 10ft.
You gain advantage on Acrobatics (DEX) checks.
You gain a bonus to any CON saving throws you make to maintain your concentration on a spell equal to your INT (minimum of +1).

6.name: Extra Attack
6.desc: You may attack twice instead of once whenever you take the @attack action on your turn. Optionally, you may cast one of your cantrips in place of one of those attacks.

10.name: Song of Defense
10.desc: You may direct your magic to absorb damage while your Bladesong is active. Whenever you take damage, you may use your reaction to expend 1 spell slot and reduce that damage to you by an amount equal to 5 times the spell slot's level.

14.name: Song of Victory
14.desc: You may add your INT (minimum of +1) to the damage of your melee weapon attacks while your Bladesong is active.

chronurgy-magic.name: Chronurgy Magic
desc: Focusing on the manipulation of time, those who follow the Chronurgy tradition learn to alter the pace of reality to their liking. Using the ramping of anticipatory dunamis energy, these mages can bend the flow of time as adroitly as a skilled musician plays an instrument, lending themselves and their allies an advantage in the blink of an eye.

2.name: Chronal Shift
2.desc: As a reaction, after you or a creature you can see within 30ft. of you makes an attack roll, ability check or saving throw, you may force the creature to reroll. You may make the decision after you see whether the roll succeeds. The target must use the result of the second roll. You may use this ability twice per long rest.

6.name: Temporal Awareness
6.desc: You may add your proficiency bonus to your initiative rolls.

10.name: Arcane Abeyance
10.desc: Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot of 4th level or lower, you may condense the spell's magic into a mote. The spell is frozen in time at the moment of casting, and held within a grey bead for 1 hour. This bead is a Tiny object, with AC 15 and 1 hitpoint, and is immune to @poison and @psychic damage. When the duration ends, or the bead is destroyed, it vanishes in a flash of light, and the spell is lost. A creature holding the bead can use its action to release the spell within, whereupon the bead disappears. The spell uses your spell attack bonus or save DC, and the spell treats the creature who released it as the caster for all other purposes. You may create a bead once per short rest.

14.name: Convergent Future
14.desc: You may peer through possible futures and magically pull one of them into events around you, ensuring a particular outcome. Whenever you or a creature you can see within 60ft. of you makes an attack roll, ability check or saving throw, you may use your reaction to ignore the die roll and decide whether the number rolled is the minimum needed to succeed, or one less than that number (your choice).

school-of-abjuration.name: School of Abjuration
desc: The School of Abjuration emphasizes magic that blocks, banishes, or protects. Detractors of this school say that its tradition is about denial, negation rather than positive assertion. You understand, however, that ending harmful effects, protecting the weak, and banishing evil influences is anything but a philosophical void. It is a proud and respected vocation. Called abjurers, members of this school are sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when important locations must be guarded against magical spying, and when portals to other planes of existence must be closed.

2.name: Abjuration Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a abjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Arcane Ward
2.desc: You may weave magic around yourself for protection. Whenever you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, you can simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself that lasts until you finish a long rest. The ward has hitpoints equal to 2 times your wizard level + INT. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 hitpoints, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 hitpoints, it cannot absorb damage, but its magic remains; whenever you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, the ward regains a number of hitpoints equal to 2 times the spell's level. You can create a ward once every long rest.

6.name: Projected Ward
6.desc: Whenever a creature that you can see within 30ft. takes damage, you may use your reaction to cause your ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 hitpoints, the warded creature takes the remaining damage.

10.name: Improved Abjuration
10.desc: Whenever you cast an abjuration spell that requires you to make an ability check as a part of casting that spell, you may add your proficiency bonus to that ability check.

14.name: Spell Resistance
14.desc: You gain advantage on saving throws against spells, and gain resistance to the damage of spells.

school-of-conjuration.name: School of Conjuration
desc: As a conjurer, you favor spells that produce objects and creatures out of thin air. You can conjure billowing clouds of killing fog or summon creatures from elsewhere to fight on your behalf. As your mastery grows, you learn spells of transportation and can teleport yourself across vast distances, even to other planes of existence, in an instant.

2.name: Conjuration Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a conjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Minor Conjuration
2.desc: You may use your action to conjure up an inanimate object in your hand, or on the ground in an unoccupied space that you can see, within 10ft. of you. This object can be no larger than 3ft. on a side, and weigh no more than 10lbs., and its form must be that of a nonmagical object that you have seen. The object is visibly magical, radiating dim light out to 5ft. The object disappears after 1 hour, when you use this feature again, or if it takes or deals any damage.

6.name: Benign Transportation
6.desc: You may use your action to teleport up to 30ft. to an unoccupied space that you can see. Alternatively, you can choose a space within range that is occupied by a Small or Medium creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places. You may use this feature once per long rest. You regain the use of this feature after casting a conjuration spell of 1st level or higher.

10.name: Focused Conjuration
10.desc: Your concentration cannot be broken as a result of taking damage while concentrating on a conjuration spell.

14.name: Durable Summons
14.desc: Any creature that you summon or create with a conjuration spell has 30 temporary hitpoints.

school-of-divination.name: School of Divination
desc: The counsel of a diviner is sought by royalty and commoners alike, for all seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness so that you can see clearly. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.

2.name: Divination Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a divnation spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Portent
2.desc: Glimpses of the future begin to press in on your awareness. Whenever you finish a long rest, roll 2d20 and record the numbers rolled. You may replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you may replace a roll in this way only once per turn. Each roll may be used only once, and you lose any unused fortelling rolls upon gaining new ones.

6.name: Expert Divination
6.desc: Casting divination spells becomes so easily to you that it expends only a fraction of your spellcasting efforts. Whenever you cast a divination spell of 2nd level or higher using a spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. This slot must be of a lower level than the spell, and cannot be higher than 5th level.

10.name: The Third Eye
10.desc: As an action, you may increase your powers of perception. Whenever you do so, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you are incapacited or until a short rest. You may use this feature once per short rest.

content.name: Darkvision
content.desc: You gain 60ft. darkvision.

content.name: Ethereal Sight
content.desc: You are able to see into the Ethereal Plane up to 60ft.

content.name: Greater Comprehension
content.desc: You are able to read any language.

content.name: See Invisibility
content.desc: You are able to see invisible creatures and objects within 10ft. of you that are within line of sight.

14.name: Greater Portent
14.desc: The visions in your dreams intensify and paint a more accurate picture in your mind of what is to come. You may roll 3d20 for your portent feature.

school-of-necromancy.name: School of Necromancy
desc: The School of Necromancy explores the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath. As you focus your studies in this tradition, you learn to manipulate the energy that animates all living things. As you progress, you learn to sap the life force from a creature as your magic destroys its body, transforming that vital energy into magical power you can manipulate. Most people see necromancers as menacing, or even villainous, due to the close association with death. Not all necromancers are evil, but the forces they manipulate are considered taboo by many societies.

2.name: Necromancy Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a necromancy spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Grim Harvest
2.desc: Once per turn, when you kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hitpoints equal to 2 times the spell's level, or 3 times if it was a necromancy spell. You don't gain this benefit for killing constructs or undead.

6.name: Undead Thralls
6.desc: You may add the @animate dead spell to your spellbook if it is not there already. Whenever you cast @animate dead, you may target one additional corpse or pile of bones, creating another zombie or skeleton as appropriate. Additonally, whenever you create an undead using a necromancy spell, the creature's hitpoint maximum is increased by an amount equal to your wizard level, and the creature may add your proficiency bonus to its weapon damage rolls.

10.name: Inured to Undeath
10.desc: You gain resistance to necrotic damage, and your hitpoint maximum cannot be reduced.

14.name: Command Undead
14.desc: As an action, you may choose 1 undead that you can see within 60ft. of you. That creature must succeed on a CHA saving throw against your wizard spell save DC. On a failure, it becomes friendly to you, and obeys your commands until you use this feature again. On a success, you cannot use this feature on that creature again. Intelligent undead are harder to control in this way. If the target has an INT score of 8 or higher, it gains advantage on the saving throw. If the target has an INT score of 12 or higher, it may repeat the saving throw at the end of every hour, ending the effect on itself on a success.
